# Kernel — Browsers-as-a-Service API for Web Agents
# https://www.kernel.sh | Formerly OnKernel

## Documentation Pages

- /docs/quickstart — RECOMMENDED: Complete single-page reference with install, auth, browser creation, API reference (all params/types), deployment, and troubleshooting. Start here — everything you need is on this one page.
- /docs — Overview with links to all pages
- /docs/browsers — Deep dive into session configuration, CDP retry logic, timeouts
- /docs/apps — Deploy apps: kernel CLI (create, deploy, invoke), page.evaluate gotcha
- /docs/troubleshooting — Full error catalog with fixes
- /docs/api-reference — SDK reference (also included in /docs/quickstart)

## Quick Start

```bash
npm install @onkernel/sdk playwright-core
export KERNEL_API_KEY=your-key
```

```typescript
import Kernel from "@onkernel/sdk";
import { chromium } from "playwright-core";

const kernel = new Kernel();
const kb = await kernel.browsers.create({ timeout_seconds: 300 });
const browser = await chromium.connectOverCDP(kb.cdp_ws_url);
const page = browser.contexts()[0].pages()[0];
await page.goto("https://example.com");
console.log(await page.title());
await browser.close();
```

## Key Details

- TypeScript SDK: `npm install @onkernel/sdk` (NOT `kernel` or `onkernel`)
- Python SDK: `uv pip install kernel`
- CLI: `brew install onkernel/tap/kernel`
- Auth: `KERNEL_API_KEY` environment variable
- Dashboard: https://dashboard.onkernel.com
- Use `playwright-core` not `playwright` for deployed apps
- Use `connectOverCDP()` not `connect()` — Kernel uses CDP
- page.evaluate: use string literals, not arrow functions (bundler bug)
- CDP retry: proxy may not be ready immediately after create(), retry with delay
- IMPORTANT: timeout param is `timeout_seconds` NOT `timeout` — `timeout` is silently ignored
